You can see here the full exchange I … Read more Read more posts Brendan o’hara mp Get in touch and keep up to date. If you’re an Argyll and Bute constituent and need to contact Brendan, you can find all contact details here . If you’d like to keep up with Brendan’s work then please subscribe to his periodic newsletter below (previous editions can be viewed here ). Newsletter VIDEO Westminster Contributions We try to feature as many Brendan’s appearances as possible and here you can find a representative example. SOCIAL CHANNELS Join Brendan Online Brendan is very active across Social Media and it’s a great way to keep up with what is happening across the Argyll and Bute Constituency and at Westminster. Below are recent posts from Brendan’s Twitter account. pages.title: About Brendan O'Hara, MP for Argyll and Bute Constituency
headings: h2: About Brendan O’Hara MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Brendan O'Hara, MP for Argyll and Bute Constituency Skip to content Menu Home News About Contact Newsletter Brendan O’Hara MP About Brendan Get in touch Newsletter About Brendan O’Hara MP Brendan lives in Helensburgh with his wife and two daughters. Originally from Glasgow, he has lived in Argyll and Bute since 2004. Educated at St. Andrew’s Secondary School in Carntyne, Brendan worked with Glasgow District Council for a few years before attending Strathclyde University, from where he graduated in 1992 with a 2:1 in Economic History and Modern History. He has twice before represented the SNP at a General Election; in Springburn in 1987 and again in Glasgow Central in 1992. Prior to his election to Parliament in May 2015, Brendan was an independent, award-winning television producer and has worked for STV, SKY SPORTS and the BBC. Among his credits are Comedy Connections and Movie Connections (BBC1), The Football Years (STV), Scotland’s Greatest Album (STV), Road To Referendum (STV) and completed the second series of David Hayman’s very successful series, On Weir’s Way , following in the footsteps of Tom Weir. pages.meta_description: How to get in touch with Brendan O'Hara MP
headings: h2: Get in touch with our friendly team:
pages.text_excerpt: Brendan O'Hara MP, Contact & Press Enquiry Details Skip to content Menu Home News About Contact Newsletter Brendan O’Hara MP Contact Brendan Newsletter Get in touch with our friendly team: Brendan has members of staff based in his constituency office who will be happy to assist constituents with any concerns you may have. You can get in touch with us directly using the brendan.ohara.mp@parliament.uk email address, by filling out the contact form below, or by calling us on 01436 670587 . Please note that I can only deal with cases from constituents who live in the Argyll and Bute UK Parliamentary constituency. Leave this field blank First name Second name Email Address Postcode Phone Comment or Message GDPR & Privacy Agreement I consent to having this website store my submitted information so that they can respond to my inquiry. pages.title: Brendan O'Hara MP questions the Foreign Secretary over the situation in Gaza | Brendan O'Hara MP
pages.meta_description: Tuesday 9th January 2024 was the first opportunity for the Foreign Affairs Select Committee to question the new Foreign Secretary David Cameron, on the situation in Gaza. You can see here the full exchange I had when I questioned him on whether he thought Israel had broken International Humanitarian law.
